stem: The perimeter of an isosceles right triangle is 26 + 26√2 inches. What is the length, in inches, of the hypotenuse of this triangle?
rationale: For an isosceles right triangle with leg L, the hypotenuse equals L√2 and the perimeter equals 2L + L√2. Setting 2L + L√2 = 26 + 26√2 and matching coefficients gives L = 13√2 (since 2·13√2 = 26√2 and 13√2 · √2 = 26). Thus the hypotenuse is L√2 = 13√2 · √2 = 26 inches. Choice B is the leg length; C is half a leg; D would result from doubling the hypotenuse.
